content: ToolBackend ABC at packages/vigor-core/src/vigor_core/interfaces.py:130-138 has no aclose() method. The Orchestrator finally block at packages/vigor-runtime/src/vigor_runtime/orchestrator.py:212-214 only awaits self._backend.aclose(). MCPToolBackend.aclose() exists at packages/vigor-mcp/src/vigor_mcp/backend.py:139-141 but is never invoked by the runtime, so every Orchestrator.run() with tools=MCPToolBackend(...) leaks one MCP subprocess or HTTP/SSE connection per server. Either (a) add aclose() to ToolBackend ABC and have orchestrator close it in finally, or (b) document that caller of Orchestrator(tools=...) owns lifecycle. Current default is silent FD/zombie-process leak.

asyncio.wait_for on session.call_tool() at packages/vigor-mcp/src/vigor_mcp/backend.py:122-130 cancels the Python awaiter when timeout_s elapses, but on stdio/SSE transports the MCP server does NOT receive a JSON-RPC notifications/cancelled message. The in-flight RPC remains live and the session's read pump can later receive that late response. The next call_tool on the same session may dequeue the prior call's response, causing silent cross-call data corruption. Bridge timeouts must propagate cancellation to the JSON-RPC layer (send notifications/cancelled with the request id) or tear down + reopen the session on timeout. Same risk applies on parent CancelledError.

description: str.startswith(prefix) used for module-name allowlists is a substring match, not a namespace boundary — accepts typosquats like 'vigor_runtime_evil' under prefix 'vigor_runtime'. Found in vigor_harness/evaluator.py:_load_factory (and a sibling copy in unmerged vigor_agent/factory.py).
resolution: Use dotted-component matching: 'module == prefix or module.startswith(prefix + ".")'. Treats Python module hierarchy as the trust boundary. Same idiom should be applied wherever a factory-path or import-path allowlist is enforced.

name: mcp-transport-split-stdio-vs-http-sse
description: VIGOR's official MCP transport policy (per ADR-0016): stdio default for local validators and stdio model critics; http/sse only for GPU-resident or stateful hosted servers (VideoScore2 wrap is the canonical example). Mixed-transport servers (quellant/openscad-mcp ships stdio+http+sse) must be configured to stdio at ToolBackend registration. Rationale: stdio-everywhere can't share GPU; http-everywhere adds auth+network surface for trivial CPU validators.

title: ADR-0017 admits pure-MCP plugins as ambient tool sources, not loop drivers
rationale: Lifting plugin_discovery's FactoryRef requirement for the tool surface only is the smallest change that closes the discoverability gap (zero-OPS-host adoption + pure-MCP is the dominant 2026 packaging shape) without violating ADR-0014/0015 typed-runtime guarantees. Option B (generic MCP-driven adapter) was rejected because no public 2026 MCP server exposes the typed compile/apply_patch contract the loop requires.

name: wait-for-timeout-rolls-back-mcp-handle
description: When asyncio.wait_for cancels an MCP call_tool on timeout, the session may be left mid-flight. The MCPToolBackend tears down the handle (closes the AsyncExitStack and clears session/cache) on TimeoutError so the next call_tool on the same server opens a fresh session.

content: MCPServerSpec.tool_allowlist materialises to a frozenset on _ServerHandle for O(1) membership checks; the list[str] on the spec stays for serialisation. Both list_tools() and call_tool() use the frozenset, never the original list, so a 500-entry allowlist costs the same as a 5-entry one.

name: fastmcp-in-tree-fixture-for-stdio-integration-tests
description: Use mcp.server.fastmcp.FastMCP in a tiny tests/_fixtures/echo_mcp_server.py and spawn it as a Python subprocess (sys.executable + script path) to drive MCPToolBackend end-to-end through the real stdio transport. Hermetic: no Node, no network, no extra deps beyond the optional 'mcp' SDK already required for the bridge itself. Pairs with pytest.importorskip('mcp') + a registered @pytest.mark.requires_mcp marker for skip-vs-deselect partitioning.

content: Custom pytest markers used in this repo MUST be registered under tool.pytest.ini_options.markers in the root pyproject.toml. The repo sets filterwarnings=['error',...] which promotes pytest's PytestUnknownMarkWarning into an exception, so an unregistered marker breaks collection. requires_mcp is registered there as the canonical example.

description: transports/sdk.py:43 evaluates 'env=dict(spec.env) if spec.env else None' — because MCPServerSpec.env is a Pydantic Field(default_factory=dict), the default is a TRUTHY-EMPTY dict {}, and 'if spec.env' is False because empty dict is FALSY in Python. So the default subprocess env is None, which means StdioServerParameters inherits the entire parent os.environ (including ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, AWS_*, etc.) into every stdio MCP subprocess. The fix is default-deny: env={'PATH': os.environ.get('PATH', '')} when spec.env is empty, mirroring vigor-adapter-video-manim/renderer.py:106.
resolution: Document as policy-only gap in docs/security/threat-model.md T1; mitigation lives in sibling task VIGOR-a29a. Threat is 'parent os.environ leaks to MCP subprocess by default' — high-risk, easy-to-trigger, asymmetric to fix.

content: vigor-mcp/transports/sdk.py:42 passes env=dict(spec.env) if spec.env else None. None means inherit-all from parent. Schema validator (vigor-core/agent_config.py:71) forbids env on http/sse and forbids headers on stdio — right shape, not redaction. Strategic-doc VIGOR-ef9a Q3 recommends drop-all-by-default ({'PATH': os.environ.get('PATH', '')}) behind a one-version DeprecationWarning.
